notes: Curated aliases are merged with official TFDA ingredient aliases by scripts/build-term-index.mjs. Keep ambiguous trade/common names, but mark them in notes so review UI can ask for confirmation.

aliases.note: Ambiguous abbreviation; can also mean butylated hydroxyanisole.

terms.notes: Common acne/keratolytic ingredient. Abbreviation BHA requires context check.
Preservative alias set. Short aliases MI/MIT are useful but should be confirmed in dense ingredient lists.
Often appears with methylisothiazolinone as an MCI/MI mixture.
Used in some preservative/antimicrobial contexts; claims can affect product classification.
Heavy-metal family matching should surface confirmation prompts for salts and compounds.
Skin-lightening context often changes risk and claim review.
Also check formaldehyde releasers as related but distinct substances.
Can be colorant or UV filter depending on function and product context.
Often used in sunscreen and soothing products.
UV filter with common INCI/common-name split.

aliases.value: フェノキシエタノール

terms.notes: High-frequency preservative alias set.
Vitamin A family names need separate handling for esters and derivatives.
Paraben family terms should not be collapsed without compound-level evidence.
Paraben family terms should not be collapsed without compound-level evidence.
Oxidizing ingredient used in hair and oral products; concentration and product type matter.
Common skin-lightening ingredient with cross-market naming variants.
Grouped because the TFDA rule covers potassium or sodium hydroxide together.
Common in hair-color and pH-adjusting contexts.
Hair dye ingredient; do not merge with substituted resorcinols without CAS evidence.
Hair dye term; sulfate salt should remain linked but identifiable.
Separate from cosmetic retinol and retinyl esters; regulatory risk differs.
Also linked to borates/tetraborates in some rules.
Family term for chloride, bromide, and saccharinate variants listed by TFDA.
Antimicrobial preservative family; salt form matters for limits.
High-frequency preservative.
Preservative/antimicrobial term with PCMX abbreviation.
Often appears in anti-dandruff or scalp-care contexts.
Formaldehyde-releasing preservative; keep linked but distinct from formaldehyde.

Formaldehyde-releasing preservative family member.
Short alias IPBC should be matched with context.
Common cooling/fragrance ingredient; rule matching may depend on product type.
Common cooling ingredient with multilingual label variants.
Taiwan food allergen labeling category for prepackaged foods.
Taiwan food allergen category. Caseinates also appear in food additive common-name material.

Taiwan food allergen category for cereals containing gluten, with common cereal aliases.
